because.text: Closes Qwen's formal-verification list. impl-rs is an independent Rust Book I (from-scratch SHA-256 FIPS 180-4 + JSON parser, zero external crates): SigmaNodeV2 serialize/deser/validate, NodeHash, CAS, genesis intrinsic, the v0.5 hash-thunk evaluator with size-priced ATP. Delivered by codex zero-relay (brief -> codex exec on an isolated clone, 76k tokens, single shot) and cold-verified by the maintainer LOCALLY on the MacBook (cargo 1.96, 1.1s build) - the first verification run under the new 'compute local, secrets remote' rule. Adversarial checks: only impl-rs/ + HANDOFF touched; genuine serialize/deser/force/step with exhaustion-before-action (not table-driven - one result_hash reference, in the vector comparison); passes a freshly-regenerated oracle suite byte-exact, so 49/49 IS the differential against the live oracle incl. the exhaustion-boundary (EV-TV4-IK-ATP0/2/3), Omega (500 ATP), R-S size-pricing and store-isolation vectors. Book I now has THREE agreeing implementations - Python oracle, warrant-go native evaluator (via ski@v1), Rust - alongside the Lean mechanization (EvalMachine: determinism/totality + memory bound). CI builds and runs the Rust conformance as a required gate. Only warrant SIGNING still runs on the mini (the seed must not travel).

subject.note: Rust Book I: third independent implementation, 49/49 conformance - the last non-Lean Qwen formal-verification ask
